spec_match.rationale: The page now targets the ChromeStatus expansion directly by probing ::backdrop, ::scroll-marker, and view-transition pseudo handles alongside baseline pseudos.

interactive.rationale: Users can open a dialog, move a scroll-marker carousel, start a real view transition, and rerun the live support matrix.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index blurb promises broad pseudo probing and fallback reporting, which the revised compatibility lab delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: The page handles missing Element.pseudo(), missing View Transition API support, null pseudo handles, thrown pseudo probes, and fallback dialog hit testing.

references.rationale: The concept includes the required https://chromestatus.com/feature/6516055192240128 reference link.

design_system.rationale: The inline CSS uses shared design variables for colors and pairs every local button background override with an explicit color.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: The major missing-reference finding is cleared, and the concept now provides a live compatibility lab for the three new CSSPseudoElement pseudo families with fallbacks.
